reasoning: The value as of 2025-03-26 was 2234. This is toward the lower end of the trailing year range (which saw highs above 7000 in early summer 2024 and lows around 1500 in September 2024). Because 2234 is somewhat low relative to the trailing 12-month average (approx 3500-4000), there is a slightly greater than 50% chance it will be higher in the coming weeks and months, assuming mean reversion within the post-2023 elevated normal baseline. The BTFP maturity didn't trigger a surge, and the recent short-term trend in March was downward from a peak of ~3300 in late February. For the nearest dates (April 6, April 29), it essentially tracks week-to-week volatility, which is symmetric but starting from a trough, yielding probabilities slightly above 0.5. For the mid-term dates (June/September), probabilities are pushed a bit higher as historical data suggests summer months previously saw elevated usage (~6000+ last mid-year). However, for the longest time horizon (March 2026), there remains a risk that borrowing decays back toward pre-2023 levels (near zero) if destigmatization efforts fail to maintain a permanent structural boost, shifting probability back closer to 0.5 or lower territory.
